```
401c15:       movss  0x7e407(%rip),%xmm6  # 480024 = 8388608
401c1d:       movaps %xmm0,%xmm2
401c20:       andps  %xmm4,%xmm2          ; xmm2 = |hit_point_z|
401c23:       ucomiss %xmm2,%xmm6         ; compare |hp.z| with 8388608
401c26:       jbe    401c54                ; if |hp.z| >= 8388608, skip floor
```

The value 8388608 = 2^23. This is checking if the float is within the range where `floorf` can be computed exactly by casting to int. This is the standard `floorf` implementation pattern.

The pattern continues:
```
401c28:       cvttss2si %xmm0,%eax        ; eax = (int)hp.z
401c2c:       pxor   %xmm2,%xmm2
401c30:       movss  0x83ea4(%rip),%xmm6  # 485adc = 1.0
401c38:       andnps %xmm0,%xmm4         ; xmm4 = sign bit of hp.z
401c3b:       cvtsi2ss %eax,%xmm2         ; xmm2 = (float)(int)hp.z
401c3f:       movaps %xmm2,%xmm1
401c42:       cmpnless %xmm0,%xmm1        ; xmm1 = (xmm2 > xmm0) ? 0xFFFFFFFF : 0
401c47:       andps  %xmm6,%xmm1         ; xmm1 = (trunc > original) ? 1.0 : 0.0
401c4a:       subss  %xmm1,%xmm2         ; xmm2 = trunc - adjustment = floor
401c4e:       orps   %xmm4,%xmm2         ; restore sign
401c51:       movaps %xmm2,%xmm0         ; xmm0 = floor(hp.z)
```

So this is `floorf(hp.z)`. Then the same is done for hp.x. Then:
```
401adc:       addsd  %xmm2,%xmm1         ; xmm1 = floor(hp.x) + floor(hp.z) as double
401ae8:       cvttsd2si %xmm1,%eax        ; eax = (int)(floor(hp.x) + floor(hp.z))
401aec:       movss  0x7d524(%rip),%xmm1  # 480018 = 0.4
401af4:       test   $0x1,%al              ; check if LSB is 1
401af6:       jne    401b00                ; if odd, use 0.4
401af8:       movss  0x7d514(%rip),%xmm1  # 480014 = 0.9
```

So the pattern is: if `((int)(floor(hp.x) + floor(hp.z))) & 1` → 0.4, else → 0.9.
